figures.caption: Figure 1: Power comparison for the normal distribution under a mean change µpost ∈ {0.5, 1, 1.5, 2} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}.

Figure 4: Power comparison for the skew normal distribution under a mean change µpost ∈ {0.5, 1, 1.5, 2} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}
Figure 8: Array CGH profile of 10 chromosomes of breast cancer cell line MDA157

Figure 3: Power comparison for the normal distribution under simultaneous changes in mean and variance, with parameters µpost ∈ {0.5, 1, 1.5, 2} and σ2 post ∈ {1.5, 2, 2.5, 3} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}

Figure 7: Power comparison for the exponential distribution under a variance parameter change σ2 post ∈ {1.5, 2, 2.5, 3} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}.

Figure 6: Power comparison for the skew normal distribution under simultaneous changes in mean, variance, and shape parameters, with µpost ∈ {0.5, 1, 1.5, 2}, σ2 post ∈ {1.5, 2, 2.5, 3}, and κpost ∈ {1.5, 2, 2.5, 3} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}.

Figure 2: Power comparison for the normal distribution under a variance change σ2 post ∈ {1.5, 2, 2.5, 3} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}.
Table 1: Empirical size comparison with nominal level α = 0.05 for various distributions (normal, skew normal, exponential) with sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200}.

Figure 5: Power comparison for the skew normal distribution under a variance change σ2 post ∈ {1.5, 2, 2.5, 3} across sample sizes n ∈ {20, 30, 50, 100, 200} and change-point locations k ∈ {0.1n, 0.2n, 0.3n, 0.4n, 0.5n}
